changes_from_v2: Added CAT-09-C: Postmarketing Requirements under 505(o)(3) (subcategory of CAT-09)
Added CAT-04-H: Clinical Site GCP Inspection Deficiencies (subcategory of CAT-04)
Added CAT-11-A: PMA device component deficiencies (subcategory of CAT-11)
Fixed miscategorization: impurity qualification studies under NONCLINICAL section now dual-tagged primary=CAT-03-G, secondary=CAT-05-A
Updated CAT-04-A boilerplate rate from 75.4% to 96.7% based on verification sample (29/30 CRLs)

categories.name: LABELING
categories.description: Deficiencies in prescribing information, container labeling, proprietary names, medication guides, and bar codes
categories.note: Labeling deficiencies are partially procedural — FDA often reserves comment on final labeling until all other issues are resolved

subcategories.name: Prescribing Information format deficiencies
subcategories.automation_method: SPL/SRPI structure validation against 201.57 required sections and format rules

subcategories.name: Carton/Container labeling deficiencies
subcategories.automation_method: Format compliance check against mandatory label elements (name, address, NDC, expiration date, lot number)

subcategories.name: Proprietary name issues
subcategories.automation_method: Similarity database cross-reference (POCA/phonetic/orthographic); final determination requires human review

subcategories.name: Medication Guide deficiencies
subcategories.automation_method: Required content section presence check per 21 CFR 208

subcategories.name: Patient labeling issues
subcategories.automation_method: Content and format validation

subcategories.name: Bar code omissions
subcategories.automation_method: NDC bar code presence check in SPL data

categories.name: FACILITY_GMP
categories.description: Manufacturing facility deficiencies including cGMP violations, Form 483 observations, pre-approval inspection failures, contamination, and data integrity issues

subcategories.name: cGMP violations (general)
subcategories.automation_method: Requires physical inspection

subcategories.name: Form 483 observations outstanding
subcategories.automation_method: Track 483 issuance/response status against FDA inspection database

subcategories.name: Pre-approval inspection (PAI) deficiencies
subcategories.automation_method: Cross-reference FDA inspection status database to verify PAI completion

subcategories.name: Contamination/sterility failures
subcategories.automation_method: Requires laboratory testing

subcategories.name: Data integrity failures
subcategories.automation_method: Requires on-site audit

categories.name: CMC_PRODUCT_QUALITY
categories.description: Chemistry, manufacturing, and controls deficiencies including drug substance characterization, stability, microbiology, container closure, analytical methods, process validation, specifications, and impurities

subcategories.name: Drug substance characterization incomplete
subcategories.automation_method: Requires chemistry review

subcategories.name: Stability data insufficient
subcategories.automation_method: Verify study duration >= claimed shelf life; check for 3 batches; verify long-term/accelerated/intermediate conditions present

subcategories.name: Microbiology/sterility issues
subcategories.automation_method: Requires microbiology expertise

subcategories.name: Container closure system deficiencies
subcategories.automation_method: Requires materials science assessment

subcategories.name: Analytical method not validated
subcategories.guidance_reference: ICH Q2(R1), FDA Analytical Procedures Validation
subcategories.automation_method: Requires scientific review of validation parameters

subcategories.name: Specifications inadequate or not justified
subcategories.automation_method: Requires scientific judgment to evaluate specification rationale

subcategories.name: Impurities not characterized
subcategories.automation_method: Requires toxicological assessment
subcategories.tagging_note: PRIMARY category when CRL NONCLINICAL section discusses impurity specifications exceeding ICH Q3B(R2) thresholds requiring toxicological qualification. Apply dual-tag: primary=CAT-03-G, secondary=CAT-05-A. The NONCLINICAL section header in these cases is misleading — the root deficiency is an impurity specification issue.

subcategories.name: Process validation incomplete
subcategories.automation_method: Check if PPQ report is present in Module 3; verify batch count

categories.name: CLINICAL
categories.description: Clinical deficiencies including efficacy failures, inadequate study design, safety signals, risk-benefit determinations, and GCP inspection findings at clinical trial sites

subcategories.name: Safety update required in response
subcategories.automation_method: Check if safety update section is present in submission per 314.50(d)(5)(vi)(b)
subcategories.note: Standard boilerplate in most CRLs — not necessarily a substantive deficiency. Verification sample (30 CRLs) shows 96.7% rate (29/30); the 75.4% estimate may undercount due to reserved-labeling-only CRLs not requiring safety updates.

subcategories.name: Failed to demonstrate efficacy
subcategories.automation_method: Requires clinical/statistical judgment

subcategories.name: Adequate and well-controlled studies lacking
subcategories.automation_method: Requires trial design review per 314.126 criteria

subcategories.name: Cardiovascular safety signal
subcategories.automation_method: Requires cardiologist review

subcategories.name: Unfavorable risk-benefit assessment
subcategories.automation_method: Requires medical judgment

subcategories.name: Hepatotoxicity/liver safety concerns
subcategories.automation_method: Requires hepatology/clinical review

subcategories.name: Postmarket study/REMS required
subcategories.automation_method: Track commitment status against FDA database

subcategories.name: Clinical site GCP inspection deficiencies
subcategories.guidance_reference: FDA GCP Guidance; ICH E6(R2) Good Clinical Practice
subcategories.automation_method: Requires review of FDA clinical site inspection reports (EIR); flag applications with for-cause GCP inspections
subcategories.description: GCP noncompliance findings at individual clinical trial sites that impact data reliability. Distinct from facility GMP inspections (CAT-02). Typically triggered by for-cause PDUFA inspections or routine BIMO inspections. Key indicators: unblinding events, protocol deviations affecting efficacy assessments, data quality issues at inspected sites, inspector observations questioning study conduct integrity.
key_distinguishing_features: Section header 'CLINICAL SITE INSPECTIONS' (distinct from 'FACILITY INSPECTIONS')
Mentions for-cause inspections of specific protocol sites
Discusses impact on efficacy data interpretability
Unblinding or protocol deviation findings at individual sites
FDA Form 483-equivalent observations for clinical sites (EIR)

categories.name: NONCLINICAL
categories.description: Nonclinical deficiencies including missing genotoxicity, carcinogenicity, reproductive toxicity, and safety pharmacology studies. NOTE: When a NONCLINICAL section in a CRL discusses impurity qualification studies required under ICH Q3B(R2), the primary category should be CAT-03-G (Impurities), not CAT-05. Apply dual-tag: primary=CAT-03-G, secondary=CAT-05-A.

subcategories.name: Genotoxicity battery incomplete
subcategories.automation_method: Check if standard battery (bacterial reverse mutation + in vitro chromosome aberration + in vivo micronucleus) is present
subcategories.tagging_note: When used as SECONDARY tag for impurity qualification (primary=CAT-03-G), count=3 miscategorized instances corrected in v2.1

subcategories.name: Carcinogenicity studies missing/inadequate
subcategories.automation_method: Apply ICH S1 decision tree based on treatment duration and drug class

subcategories.name: Reproductive/developmental toxicity missing
subcategories.automation_method: Check if required based on patient population and indication

subcategories.name: Safety pharmacology battery missing
subcategories.automation_method: Check if core battery (CNS, cardiovascular, respiratory) studies are present in Module 4

categories.name: CLINICAL_PHARMACOLOGY
categories.description: Clinical pharmacology deficiencies including missing DDI studies, organ impairment studies, QT studies, and food effect studies

subcategories.name: Drug-drug interaction studies missing
subcategories.automation_method: Cross-reference metabolic pathway (CYP profile in Module 3) against required DDI study list

subcategories.name: Renal/hepatic impairment study missing
subcategories.automation_method: Check excretion pathway; if >= 30% renal elimination, verify renal impairment study present

subcategories.name: QT/TQT study deficiencies
subcategories.automation_method: Check if TQT study report or E14 concentration-QT analysis/waiver present

subcategories.name: Food effect study missing
subcategories.automation_method: For oral dosage forms, verify food effect study present in Module 5.3.1

categories.name: HUMAN_FACTORS
categories.description: Human factors/usability deficiencies for combination products and delivery devices

subcategories.name: Human factors study not conducted
subcategories.automation_method: Check if product is combination product; if so, verify HF report present

subcategories.name: Use-related risks not addressed
subcategories.automation_method: Requires ergonomics/usability expert review

categories.name: BIOEQUIVALENCE
categories.description: Bioequivalence study design, missing strength studies, and dissolution deficiencies

subcategories.name: BE study design inadequate
subcategories.automation_method: Requires scientific review

subcategories.name: BE study missing for required strengths
subcategories.automation_method: Cross-reference dosage strengths in Module 3 against BE studies in Module 5

subcategories.name: In vitro dissolution data inadequate
subcategories.automation_method: Requires scientific judgment

categories.name: ADMINISTRATIVE_REGULATORY
categories.description: Administrative deficiencies including patent certifications, exclusivity issues, and formal postmarketing study/clinical trial requirements under 505(o)(3)
categories.note: total_crls_affected updated from 11 to 28 to include CAT-09-C PMR/PMC records

subcategories.name: Patent certification issues
subcategories.automation_method: Completeness check for Paragraph IV certifications against Orange Book patents

subcategories.name: Exclusivity issues
subcategories.automation_method: Cross-reference exclusivity database for blocking exclusivities

subcategories.name: Postmarketing requirements/commitments (PMR/PMC) under 505(o)(3)
subcategories.guidance_reference: FDA Guidance for Postmarketing Studies and Clinical Trials under 505(o)(3)
subcategories.automation_method: Detect 'POSTMARKETING REQUIREMENTS UNDER 505(o)(3)' section header in CRL text; extract required study type (carcinogenicity, PK, clinical registry, etc.) and timetable
subcategories.description: Formal statutory postmarketing study requirements issued under section 505(o)(3) of the FDCA. Distinguished from general postmarket study commitments by the formal statutory citation and finding that passive surveillance (505(k)(1)) and the active pharmacovigilance system (505(k)(3)) are insufficient to assess identified serious risks. Appears as a formal titled section in CRL — 'POSTMARKETING REQUIREMENTS UNDER 505(o)(3)'. Studies are legally binding upon approval (unlike PMC postmarketing commitments, which are voluntary). Common triggers: carcinogenicity uncertainty, drug-drug interaction risks requiring in vivo confirmation, rare serious risks needing prospective registries.
key_distinguishing_features: Explicit citation of FD&C Act 505(o)(3) in section header
FDA statement that 505(k)(1) passive surveillance is insufficient
FDA statement that 505(k)(3) active pharmacovigilance is insufficient
Common study types: carcinogenicity (rat/mouse), PK DDI studies, patient registries
Timetable and annual reporting requirements referenced
subcategories.boundary_with_cat04g: CAT-09-C covers formal statutory PMRs under 505(o)(3) with explicit finding language. CAT-04-G covers REMS requirements and general postmarket study requests without 505(o)(3) citation.

categories.name: PEDIATRIC_PREA
categories.description: Missing required pediatric assessments under PREA

subcategories.name: Required pediatric assessments missing
subcategories.automation_method: Check if waiver, deferral, or pediatric study plan has been submitted and agreed upon

categories.name: DEVICE_COMBINATION_PRODUCT
categories.description: Deficiencies specific to the device component of drug-device combination products reviewed under NDA/BLA. The drug application cannot be approved until the device component PMA (Premarket Approval) or 510(k) deficiencies are resolved by CDRH. Distinct from Human Factors deficiencies (CAT-07), which address usability study adequacy. CAT-11 addresses the regulatory status of the device component itself.
categories.note: This category is uniquely cross-agency: deficiencies originate from FDA's Center for Devices and Radiological Health (CDRH) via the combination product coordination process (21 CFR Part 3). The NDA/BLA CRL reflects but does not generate these deficiencies.

subcategories.name: PMA device component deficiencies blocking NDA/BLA approval
subcategories.guidance_reference: FDA Combination Products Guidance; CDRH PMA Guidance
subcategories.automation_method: Detect combination product indicator in application type and check CDRH PMA/510(k) approval status in FDA device database; flag if device component has outstanding Not Approvable/Deficiency letter
subcategories.description: The device component of a combination product has received a Not Approvable letter, outstanding deficiencies, or incomplete PMA submission from CDRH. The drug component NDA/BLA may be otherwise adequate but cannot be approved until CDRH resolves the device deficiencies. CRL cites the CDRH letter date and outstanding device-specific deficiencies. Often co-occurs with CAT-07 (Human Factors) since HF deficiencies for the device interface are tracked by CDRH under 21 CFR 820.30.
subcategories.section_header_in_crl: CLINICAL (embedded) or standalone device deficiency section
key_distinguishing_features: Cites 'Not Approvable letter' from CDRH with date
References 'Center for Devices and Radiological Health (CDRH)'
States NDA/BLA 'cannot be approved' until device is approvable
May reference '21 CFR 820.30' for device design controls
subcategories.boundary_with_cat07: CAT-11-A = device component PMA regulatory status blocking approval (CDRH decision). CAT-07 = human factors/usability study adequacy for the device interface (FDA drug review concern). Both may apply simultaneously to the same CRL.

corrections_applied.pattern: Impurity qualification studies placed under NONCLINICAL section header
corrections_applied.root_cause: FDA CRL letters sometimes place impurity qualification discussions under a 'NONCLINICAL' section header when they require toxicological qualification studies per ICH Q3B(R2). The section header is misleading — the root deficiency is an impurity specification issue, not a standalone nonclinical study gap.
corrections_applied.detection_signal: NONCLINICAL section header + ICH Q3B(R2) citation + language about impurity levels exceeding qualification thresholds
corrections_applied.rule: When a CRL NONCLINICAL section discusses impurity specifications exceeding ICH Q3B(R2) thresholds and requires toxicological qualification data, assign primary=CAT-03-G, secondary=CAT-05-A. If the nonclinical section discusses standalone safety study gaps (genotoxicity, carcinogenicity, reproductive tox) without impurity framing, assign CAT-05-A alone.

updated_counts.note: Updated from v2.0 (41 subcategories) — 4 new subcategories added: CAT-04-H, CAT-09-C, CAT-11-A (new parent CAT-11 counted as 1)
